---
title: "Stated Preference Elicitation"
subtitle: "April 2026 Department Meeting"
editor: visual
format: 
  revealjs: 
    theme: simple
    transition: fade
    chalkboard: true
    slide-number: true
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Other Broad Research Methods Themes
- Consumer demand and welfare analysis
- Spatial econometrics
- Surrogate (meta)-models for complex simulation systems
- Decision-making under uncertainty
- System resilience

## Revealed Preferences
What we observe people doing with their time and money.

![](./images/calgary_road_rp.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}

## Stated preferences
What people would like to be doing with their time and money.

![](./images/oulu_bike_sp.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}

## Stated Preference Experiments {.smaller}

<div id="sp-container">
  <div id="task-content"></div>
</div>

<script>
// -----------------------------
// 1. Attribute levels
// -----------------------------
const materials = ["Cross‑Laminated Timber (CLT)", "Steel Frame", "Reinforced Concrete"];
const insulation = ["Mineral Wool", "Spray Foam", "Cellulose"];
const carbon = ["Low", "Medium", "High"];
const cost = ["Low", "Moderate", "High"];

// -----------------------------
// 2. Random profile generator
// -----------------------------
function generateProfile() {
  return {
    material: materials[Math.floor(Math.random()*materials.length)],
    insulation: insulation[Math.floor(Math.random()*insulation.length)],
    carbon: carbon[Math.floor(Math.random()*carbon.length)],
    cost: cost[Math.floor(Math.random()*cost.length)]
  };
}

// -----------------------------
// 3. Render a choice task
// -----------------------------
let taskIndex = 0;
const totalTasks = 4;
let responses = [];

function renderTask() {
  const content = document.getElementById("task-content");

  // After last task: do NOTHING (no DOM changes)
  if (taskIndex >= totalTasks) {
    return;
  }

  const alts = [generateProfile(), generateProfile(), generateProfile()];

  content.innerHTML = `
    <h4>Choice Task ${taskIndex+1} of ${totalTasks}</h4>
    <p>Select the building option you prefer.</p>

    <form id="choice-form">
      <div class="choice-set">
        ${alts.map((alt, i) => `
          <label class="alt-card">
            <input type="radio" name="choice" value="${i}">
            <ul>
              <li><strong>Material:</strong> ${alt.material}</li>
              <li><strong>Insulation:</strong> ${alt.insulation}</li>
              <li><strong>Embodied Carbon:</strong> ${alt.carbon}</li>
              <li><strong>Cost:</strong> ${alt.cost}</li>
            </ul>
          </label>
        `).join("")}
      </div>

      <label class="none-option">
        <input type="radio" name="choice" value="none">
        None of the options
      </label>

      <button type="submit" class="submit-btn">Submit Choice</button>
    </form>
  `;

  const form = document.getElementById("choice-form");

  form.addEventListener("submit", function(e) {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();          // <-- CRITICAL FIX
    e.stopImmediatePropagation(); // <-- EXTRA SAFETY

    const selected = document.querySelector('input[name="choice"]:checked');
    if (!selected) return;

    const value = selected.value;
    responses.push({
      task: taskIndex+1,
      choice: value === "none" ? "None" : Number(value)+1,
      profile: value === "none" ? null : alts[value]
    });

    taskIndex++;

    // Only re-render if more tasks remain
    if (taskIndex < totalTasks) {
      renderTask();
    }
  });
}

// -----------------------------
// Initialize
// -----------------------------
renderTask();
</script>

<style>
.choice-set {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.alt-card {
  border: 1px solid #ccc;
  padding: 1rem;
  width: 30%;
  background: #fafafa;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s, border-color 0.2s;
}

.alt-card:hover {
  background: #eef6ff;
  border-color: #4a90e2;
}

.alt-card input[type="radio"] {
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}

.none-option {
  display: block;
  margin: 1rem 0;
  font-weight: bold;
}

.submit-btn {
  padding: 0.6rem 1.2rem;
  font-size: 1rem;
  border-radius: 4px;
}
</style>

## Current Applications
- Housing and neighbhourhood choice in Alberta
- Housing location choice in Singapore -  with Prateek Bansal at National University of Singapore
- Long-distance EV choice - with Sven Anders at University of Alberta
- Causal inference in chronic wasting disease and hunting location choice - with Vic Adamowicz at University of Alberta
- Child-parent treatment choice + surgeon choice - with Deborah Marshall at University of Calgary

## Workshop on Measurement and Methods in Choice Behaviour {.smaller}
- May 2-3: Workshop at Banff International Research Station (BIRS)
    - Scholars from across Canada in transportation, environment, health, marketing, and industrial organization
- May 1: Seminar on water economics with Weizhe Weng (University of Guelph)
- May 4: Seminar on travel behaviour with Adam Weiss (Carleton University) and Khandker Nurul Habib (University of Toronto)
